title: Bash/Shell Scripting - The Complete A-Z Reference
description: Complete shell scripting reference: variables, control flow, functions, text processing, automation, and best practices

commands.name: Script Structure & Shebang
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano hello.sh
#!/bin/bash
echo "Hello World"
john@pc:~$ bash hello.sh
Hello World

commands.name: Variables
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano var.sh
#!/bin/bash
name="John"
echo "Hello $name"
john@pc:~$ bash var.sh
Hello John

john@pc:~$ readonly PI=3.14
john@pc:~$ PI=3.15
bash: PI: readonly variable

commands.name: Special Variables
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano args.sh
#!/bin/bash
echo "Script: $0"
echo "First arg: $1"
echo "All args: $@"
echo "Count: $#"

Script: args.sh
First arg: hello
All args: hello world
Count: 2

john@pc:~$ ls /
bin  home  usr
john@pc:~$ echo "Exit: $?"
0
john@pc:~$ ls /nonexist 2>/dev/null
john@pc:~$ echo "Exit: $?"
2

john@pc:~$ echo "Current PID: $$
12345
john@pc:~$ sleep 5 &
[1] 12346
john@pc:~$ echo "Background PID: $!"

commands.name: Parameter Expansion - Default Values

echo "${name:=Guest}"
Guest
john@pc:~$ echo "$name"
Guest

john@pc:~$ unset name
echo "${name:?Error: name not set}"

:+ returns alt if var is set

Hello John

commands.name: String Manipulation
Indexed Arrays
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano for-array.sh
#!/bin/bash
# Define an array
names=("Nabil" "Minhaz" "Fazlul" "Yasin")

# Loop through the array
for name in "${names[@]}"; do
  echo "Hello, $name!"
done
john@pc:~$ bash for-array.sh
Hello, Nabil!
Hello, Minhaz!
Hello, Fazlul!
Hello, Yasin!

john@pc:~$ names=("a" "b" "c")
john@pc:~$ unset names[1]
john@pc:~$ echo ${names[@]}
a c
for f in "${fruits[@]}"; do echo "$f"; done

john@pc:~$ fruits=("Apple" "Banana" "Cherry")
john@pc:~$ for f in "${fruits[@]}"; do echo "$f"; done
Apple
Banana
Cherry

commands.name: Associative Arrays (Bash 4+)
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano user.sh
#!/bin/bash
declare -A user
user[name]="John"
user[age]=25
user[city]="NYC"
echo "Name: ${user[name]}"
echo "Age: ${user[age]}"
echo "City: ${user[city]}"
john@pc:~$ bash user.sh
Name: John
Age: 25
City: NYC

john@pc:~$ declare -A user
user[name]="John" user[age]=25
john@pc:~$ echo ${!user[@]}
name age

commands.name: Array Operations - Advanced

commands.name: if/elif/else
rows:

#!/bin/bash
if [ "$1" = "hello" ]; then
  echo "Hi there!"
fi

Hi there!

john@pc:~$ nano check-age.sh
#!/bin/bash
age=$1
if [ $age -ge 18 ]; then
  echo "Adult ($age)"
else
  echo "Minor ($age)"
fi
john@pc:~$ bash check-age.sh 20
Adult (20)
john@pc:~$ bash check-age.sh 15
Minor (15)
if [ -f "$1" ]; then echo "File"; elif [ -d "$1" ]; then echo "Dir"; fi

john@pc:~$ nano check-file.sh
#!/bin/bash
path="$1"
if [ -f "$path" ]; then
  echo "File: $path"
elif [ -d "$path" ]; then
  echo "Directory: $path"
else
  echo "Not found: $path"
fi

john@pc:~$ bash check-file.sh /etc

commands.name: Loop Control & Select
rows: for i in {1..10}; do [[ $i -eq 5 ]] && break; echo "$i"; done

select opt in "List" "Quit"; do case $opt in List) ls;; Quit) break;; esac; done
for word in $(cat file.txt); do echo "$word"; done
while IFS= read -r -d "" file; do echo "$file"; done < <(find . -name "*.txt" -print0)

commands.name: Argument Parsing (getopts)
rows: while getopts "f:o:v" opt; do case $opt in f) file=$OPTARG;; o) output=$OPTARG;; v) verbose=true;; esac; done

john@pc:~$ nano parser.sh
#!/bin/bash
while getopts "f:o:v" opt; do
  case $opt in
    f) file="$OPTARG" ;;
    o) output="$OPTARG" ;;
    v) verbose=true ;;
  esac
done
echo "file: $file"
echo "output: $output"
echo "verbose: ${verbose:-false}"
john@pc:~$ bash parser.sh -f input.txt -o out.txt -v
file: input.txt
output: out.txt
verbose: true

commands.name: Complete Backup Script

commands.name: 1. Database Backup Automation
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Automated MySQL/PostgreSQL backup with verification
set -euo pipefail

DB_USER="root"
DB_NAME="myapp"
BACKUP_DIR="/backup/db"
RETENTION_DAYS=7
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

# Backup with compression
mysqldump -u "$DB_USER" "$DB_NAME" | gzip > "$BACKUP_DIR/${DB_NAME}_${TIMESTAMP}.sql.gz"

# Verify backup is not empty
BACKUP_FILE="$BACKUP_DIR/${DB_NAME}_${TIMESTAMP}.sql.gz"
if [[ ! -s "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Backup file is empty!" | mail -s "BACKUP FAILED" admin@example.com
  exit 1
fi

# Show backup size
SIZE=$(du -h "$BACKUP_FILE" | cut -f1)
echo "Backup created: $BACKUP_FILE ($SIZE)"

# Remote sync to offsite storage
rsync -avz "$BACKUP_DIR/" backup@offsite:/backups/db/

# Clean old backups (local)
find "$BACKUP_DIR" -name "*.sql.gz" -mtime +$RETENTION_DAYS -delete
echo "Cleaned backups older than $RETENTION_DAYS days"

# Cron: 0 2 * * * /usr/local/bin/db-backup.sh

commands.name: 2. Server Health Monitor & Slack Alert
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Monitor disk, memory, CPU, services. Alert via Slack.
set -euo pipefail

HOSTNAME=$(hostname)
SLACK_WEBHOOK="https://hooks.slack.com/services/YOUR/WEBHOOK"

send_alert() {
  command -v jq &>/dev/null || { echo "jq required for Slack alerts" >&2; exit 1; }
  local message="$1"
  local payload
  payload=$(jq -n --arg h "$HOSTNAME" --arg m "$message" \
    '{"text":"[\($h)] \($m)"}') || { echo "Failed to build JSON payload" >&2; exit 1; }
  curl -s -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
    --data "$payload" \
    "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" &>/dev/null
}

# --- Disk Check ---
df -h | awk 'NR>1 {print $5, $6}' | while read -r use mount; do
  pct=${use%\%}
  if [[ $pct -gt 90 ]]; then
    send_alert "CRITICAL: Disk $mount is $use full!"
  elif [[ $pct -gt 80 ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: $mount at $use" | logger -t monitor
  fi
done

# --- Memory Check ---
MEM_FREE=$(free -m | awk '/Mem:/ {print $7}')
MEM_TOTAL=$(free -m | awk '/Mem:/ {print $2}')
MEM_PCT=$(( (MEM_TOTAL - MEM_FREE) * 100 / MEM_TOTAL ))
if [[ $MEM_PCT -gt 90 ]]; then
  send_alert "CRITICAL: Memory at ${MEM_PCT}%!"
fi

# --- Service Health Check ---
SERVICES=("nginx" "postgresql" "redis-server" "ssh")
for svc in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
  if ! systemctl is-active --quiet "$svc"; then
    echo "Restarting $svc..."
    systemctl restart "$svc"
    send_alert "$svc was down and restarted"
  fi
done

# --- SSL Certificate Expiry ---
expiry_date=$(echo | openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -enddate 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2)
if [[ -n "$expiry_date" ]]; then
  expiry_epoch=$(date -d "$expiry_date" +%s)
  now_epoch=$(date +%s)
  days_left=$(( (expiry_epoch - now_epoch) / 86400 ))
  if [[ $days_left -lt 7 ]]; then
    send_alert "SSL cert expires in $days_left days!"
  fi
fi

commands.name: 3. Zero-Downtime Deployment
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Deploy web app with zero downtime via symlink swap
set -euo pipefail

APP_NAME="myapp"
REPO="git@github.com:org/${APP_NAME}.git"
DEPLOY_DIR="/opt/${APP_NAME}"
RELEASES_DIR="/opt/${APP_NAME}_releases"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
RELEASE_DIR="${RELEASES_DIR}/${TIMESTAMP}"

mkdir -p "$RELEASE_DIR"
cd "$RELEASE_DIR"
git clone --depth 1 "$REPO" .
npm ci --production
npm run build
cp "${DEPLOY_DIR}/.env" . 2>/dev/null || true

# Atomic symlink swap
ln -sfn "$RELEASE_DIR" "${DEPLOY_DIR}_new"
mv -T "${DEPLOY_DIR}_new" "$DEPLOY_DIR"
systemctl reload "$APP_NAME" 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart "$APP_NAME"

# Health check
sleep 5
if curl -sf http://localhost/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Deployment successful: $TIMESTAMP"
else
  echo "Health check failed! Rolling back..."
  PREV=$(ls -dt "${RELEASES_DIR}"/* | head -2 | tail -1)
  ln -sfn "$PREV" "$DEPLOY_DIR"
  systemctl restart "$APP_NAME"
  exit 1
fi

# Keep last 3 releases
ls -dt "${RELEASES_DIR}"/* | tail -n +4 | xargs rm -rf

commands.name: 4. Log Rotation & Cleanup
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Compress large logs, archive, cleanup old ones
set -euo pipefail

LOG_DIRS=("/var/log/nginx" "/var/log/myapp")
ARCHIVE_BASE="/var/log/archive"
RETENTION_DAYS=30
MAX_LOG_SIZE_MB=500

for log_dir in "${LOG_DIRS[@]}"; do
  [[ -d "$log_dir" ]] || continue
  YEAR_MONTH=$(date +%Y/%m)
  ARCHIVE_DIR="${ARCHIVE_BASE}/${YEAR_MONTH}"
  mkdir -p "$ARCHIVE_DIR"

  find "$log_dir" -name "*.log" -type f -size +${MAX_LOG_SIZE_MB}M | while read -r log; do
    BASENAME=$(basename "$log")
    gzip -c "$log" > "${ARCHIVE_DIR}/${BASENAME}.$(date +%Y%m%d).gz"
    : > "$log"  # truncate original
    echo "Rotated: $log"
  done
done

find "$ARCHIVE_BASE" -name "*.gz" -type f -mtime +$RETENTION_DAYS -delete
echo "Archive cleaned"

commands.name: 5. Batch User Management
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bulk user operations from CSV
set -euo pipefail

ACTION="${1:-}"
CSV="${2:-users.csv}"

usage() {
  echo "Usage: $0 {create|delete|list} [csv-file]"
  echo "CSV: username,fullname,group,shell"
  exit 1
}

[[ -n "$ACTION" ]] || usage

case "$ACTION" in
  create)
    [[ -f "$CSV" ]] || { echo "File not found"; exit 1; }
    while IFS=',' read -r username fullname group shell; do
      [[ "$username" == "username" ]] && continue
      id "$username" &>/dev/null && { echo "SKIP: $username exists"; continue; }
      useradd -m -s "$shell" -c "$fullname" -U "$username"
      usermod -aG "$group" "$username"
      PASS=$(openssl rand -base64 12)
      echo "$username:$PASS" | chpasswd
      echo "CREATED: $username - Pass: $PASS"
    done < "$CSV"
    ;;
  delete)
    shift 2
    for user in "$@"; do userdel -r "$user" && echo "Deleted $user"; done
    ;;
  list)
    awk -F: '$3 >= 1000 {printf "%s (UID: %s)\n", $1, $3}' /etc/passwd
    ;;
  *) usage ;;
esac

commands.name: 6. SSL Auto-Renewal
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Renew Let's Encrypt SSL and reload services
set -euo pipefail

LOGFILE="/var/log/ssl-renew.log"
log() { echo "[$(date +%Y-%m-%d)] $*" >> "$LOGFILE"; }

for domain in "$@"; do
  expiry=$(echo | openssl s_client -connect "${domain}:443" -servername "$domain" 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -enddate 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2)
  [[ -z "$expiry" ]] && { log "Cannot check $domain"; continue; }
  days_left=$(( ($(date -d "$expiry" +%s) - $(date +%s)) / 86400 ))
  log "$domain: $days_left days left"
  [[ $days_left -lt 30 ]] || continue
  certbot certonly --nginx -d "$domain" --non-interactive --agree-tos -m admin@example.com \
    && log "Renewed $domain" || log "FAILED $domain"
done
systemctl reload nginx || systemctl restart nginx

commands.name: 7. Docker Cleanup & Health Check
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Docker housekeeping: prune containers, images, volumes
set -euo pipefail

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*"; }
log "=== Docker Cleanup ==="

# Remove stopped containers
docker container prune -f 2>/dev/null && log "Removed stopped containers"

# Remove unused images (24h+)
docker image prune -a -f --filter "until=24h" 2>/dev/null && log "Cleaned old images"

# Remove unused volumes
docker volume prune -f 2>/dev/null && log "Cleaned unused volumes"

# Check restarting containers
docker ps -a --format "{{.Names}} {{.Status}}" | while read -r name status; do
  echo "$status" | grep -q "Restarting" && log "ALERT: $name restarting!"
done

# Limit container log sizes
find /var/lib/docker/containers -name "*-json.log" -exec truncate -s 50M {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
log "=== Complete ==="

commands.name: 8. AWS EC2 Auto-Stop (Cost Optimizer)
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Stop tagged EC2 instances after hours to save costs
set -euo pipefail

TAG_KEY="AutoStop"
TAG_VALUE="true"

# Only run at 8 PM (adjust hour as needed)
[[ $(date +%H) -eq 20 ]] || exit 0

INSTANCES=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --filters "Name=tag:${TAG_KEY},Values=${TAG_VALUE}" \
            "Name=instance-state-name,Values=running" \
  --query "Reservations[*].Instances[*].InstanceId" \
  --output text)

if [[ -n "$INSTANCES" ]]; then
  aws ec2 stop-instances --instance-ids $INSTANCES --region "${AWS_REGION:-us-east-1}"
fi

# Delete old snapshots (7 days+)
SEVEN_DAYS_AGO=$(date -d '-7 days' --iso-8601)
for snap in $(aws ec2 describe-snapshots --owner-ids self \
  --query "Snapshots[?StartTime<='$SEVEN_DAYS_AGO'].SnapshotId" \
  --output text); do
  aws ec2 delete-snapshot --snapshot-id "$snap" --region "${AWS_REGION:-us-east-1}" || true
done

commands.name: 9. Error Log Analysis & Email Report
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Parse logs, extract errors, email report
set -euo pipefail

REPORT="/tmp/error-report-$(date +%F).txt"

{
  echo "Error Report: $(date)"
  echo "Host: $(hostname)"
  echo ""
  echo "--- Nginx 5xx Errors (24h) ---"
  grep "$(date -d '1 day ago' +%d/%b/%Y):" /var/log/nginx/access.log 2>/dev/null | grep -c '" 5[0-9][0-9] ' || echo "0"
  echo ""
  echo "--- Top 10 Application Errors ---"
  grep -oP 'ERROR: \K.*' /var/log/myapp/error.log 2>/dev/null | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10 || echo "(none)"
  echo ""
  echo "--- Failed SSH Logins (today) ---"
  grep "$(date +%b\ %e)" /var/log/auth.log 2>/dev/null | grep "Failed password" | awk '{print $11}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5 || echo "(none)"
  echo ""
  echo "--- Disks > 85% Usage ---"
  df -h | awk '$5+0 > 85 {print $1, $5, $6}'
  echo ""
  echo "--- OOM Killer Events ---"
  dmesg -T 2>/dev/null | grep -i "oom-killer" | tail -3 || echo "(none)"
} > "$REPORT"

# Email the report
mail -s "Error Report - $(hostname) - $(date +%F)" admin@example.com < "$REPORT"

# Also alert if critical
if grep -q "OOM\|CRITICAL" "$REPORT" 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "Critical errors found on $(hostname)!" | mail -s "ALERT: Critical Error" admin@example.com
fi

commands.name: 10. Git Sync & Maintenance
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Bulk git pull, status check across all projects
set -euo pipefail

BASE_DIR="/home/projects"
LOG="/var/log/git-sync.log"

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Git Sync ==="

for repo in "$BASE_DIR"/*/; do
  [[ -d "${repo}.git" ]] || continue
  cd "$repo"
  NAME=$(basename "$repo")
  BRANCH=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
  
  # Stash any local changes before pulling
  git stash push -m "auto-stash $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" --quiet 2>/dev/null || true
  
  if git pull --rebase origin "$BRANCH" 2>/dev/null; then
    log "OK: $NAME [$BRANCH]"
  else
    log "FAILED: $NAME [$BRANCH]"
  fi
  
  # Check if we have unpushed commits
  AHEAD=$(git rev-list --count "@{u}..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  if [[ $AHEAD -gt 0 ]]; then
    log "PENDING: $NAME is $AHEAD commits ahead of remote"
  fi
done

log "=== Complete ==="

commands.name: 11. Central Cron Orchestrator
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Central cron manager with logging, locking, and alerting
set -euo pipefail

LOG_DIR="/var/log/cron-jobs"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

run_job() {
  local name=$1 cmd=$2
  local log="${LOG_DIR}/${name}_$(date +%Y%m%d).log"
  local lock="/tmp/${name}.lock"
  
  # Prevent concurrent runs
  exec 8>"$lock"
  flock -n 8 || { echo "SKIP: $name already running" >> "$log"; return 1; }
  
  echo "=== $(date): Starting $name ===" >> "$log"
  START=$(date +%s)
  
  if eval "$cmd" >> "$log" 2>&1; then
    echo "=== SUCCESS ($(( $(date +%s) - START ))s) ===" >> "$log"
  else
    echo "=== FAILED (exit=$?, duration=$(( $(date +%s) - START ))s) ===" >> "$log"
    echo "Cron job $name failed on $(hostname)!" | mail -s "CRON ALERT" admin@example.com
  fi
  
  flock -u 8
}

# Define your scheduled jobs
run_job "db-backup"   "/usr/local/bin/db-backup.sh"
run_job "ssl-renew"   "/usr/local/bin/renew-ssl.sh"
run_job "docker-clean" "/usr/local/bin/docker-cleanup.sh"
run_job "log-rotate"  "/usr/local/bin/log-rotate.sh"

commands.name: 12. Network Connectivity Probe
commands.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Test connectivity to critical services and report
set -euo pipefail

# Format: name host port
SERVICES=(
  "Database"      "db.example.com"     5432
  "Redis"         "redis.example.com"  6379
  "API"           "api.example.com"    443
  "Web"           "example.com"        443
  "DNS"           "8.8.8.8"            53
)

PASS=0
FAIL=0
FAILED=""

{
  echo "Network Probe Report: $(date)"
  echo "From: $(hostname) ($(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}'))"
  echo ""
  
  for ((i=0; i<${#SERVICES[@]}; i+=3)); do
    name="${SERVICES[$i]}"
    host="${SERVICES[$((i+1))]}"
    port="${SERVICES[$((i+2))]}"
    
    if timeout 5 bash -c "echo > /dev/tcp/$host/$port" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "  OK: $name ($host:$port)"
      ((PASS++))
    else
      echo "  FAIL: $name ($host:$port)"
      ((FAIL++))
      FAILED+="$name "
    fi
  done
  
  echo ""
  echo "Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed"
} | tee /var/log/network-probe.log

# Alert if any failed
if [[ $FAIL -gt 0 ]]; then
  echo "Network probe alert: $FAILED unreachable on $(hostname)" | mail -s "NETWORK ALERT" admin@example.com
fi
